message:
feat(repair): implement repair policy configuration, CLI resolution, and
extension system improvements [bd-k5q5.9.1.1]

Repair policy subsystem:
- Add RepairPolicyMode enum (Off, Suggest, AutoSafe, AutoStrict) to
  control how the agent handles broken or incompatible extensions
- Add RepairPolicyConfig and ResolvedRepairPolicy structs with full
  explainability metadata (requested_mode, effective_mode, source)
- Implement precedence resolution: CLI --repair-policy flag > PI_REPAIR_POLICY
  env var > settings.json repairPolicy.mode > default ("suggest")
- Add --repair-policy and --explain-repair-policy CLI flags with tests
  for all flag variants (off, auto-safe, absent, explain)
- Add print_resolved_repair_policy() JSON output for introspection
- Wire config merge to propagate repair_policy through Config::merge()

Import parser rewrite (extensions.rs):
- Replace regex-heavy extract_import_specifiers with a hand-rolled
  parse_top_level_import_specifier that correctly handles:
  - Side-effect imports: import "pkg"
  - Standard imports: import X from "pkg"
  - import type ... from "pkg" (TypeScript)
  - Leading whitespace and identifier-continue disambiguation
- Refactor extract_import_specifiers to call the new parser first,
  then fall back to regex for side-effect and dynamic imports with
  dedup to prevent duplicate specifier entries
- Change load_one_extension to call add_extension_root_with_id()
  passing the extension ID for better root tracking

Truncation optimization (tools.rs):
- Remove TailLine enum; truncate_tail now scans line boundaries from
  the end using memchr::memrchr instead of collecting into Vec and
  reversing — avoids per-line allocation/reversal/join overhead
- Single slice start offset tracks the result region; only allocates
  in the partial-line fallback case

Code style:
- Convert PiJsRuntimeConfig construction in extensions_js.rs tests
  from field mutation to struct literal with ..Default syntax
- Apply rustfmt to assert_eq! macros in security_fs_escape.rs tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
